title: calochortus umpquaensis subsp. flavicomus
intro: Calochortus umpquaensis subsp. flavicomus, also known as the Umpqua mariposa lily, is a rare and endemic subspecies of flowering plant native to the Umpqua River basin in Oregon, USA. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Calochortus umpquaensis subsp. flavicomus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, but further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
